description: Phase 5 - Complete threading infrastructure: Add request cancellation, duplicate prevention, timing instrumentation, and proper per-thread database connections to the async report system. The existing DatabaseTask QRunnable already moves work off the GUI thread, but lacks cancellation tokens, duplicate request guards, and timing metrics.
steps: 1. In native/src/app/QmlBackend.h, add a private ReportRequestTracker class (or struct) that tracks: (a) a QAtomicInt generation counter per task type, (b) a QElapsedTimer for timing instrumentation, (c) a bool flag indicating if a request of that type is already in-flight.

2. In the DatabaseTask class in native/src/app/QmlBackend.cpp, add a generation parameter. Before posting results via QMetaObject::invokeMethod, check if the generation matches the current tracker value - if not, discard the stale result silently.

3. In each fetchX() method in QmlBackend.cpp, increment the generation counter for that task type before submitting the task. If a request is already in-flight for the same type with different params, the old one becomes stale automatically via generation mismatch.

4. Add timing instrumentation to DatabaseTask::run(). Record: request_start (set in fetchX), db_query_start, db_query_end, transform_start, transform_end. Pass timing data back alongside the result via a QVariantMap 'timing' field.
5. In each onXTaskFinished slot, extract the timing data and emit it via a new signal reportTimingComplete(const QString& reportName, const QVariantMap& timings) for diagnostics.

6. Verify that SqliteBusinessServices creates a fresh SqliteDb (with QUuid connection name) per method call - this is already correct for thread safety. For SqlBusinessServices (ODBC), verify ConnectionPool::acquire() uses per-thread connection names (it already includes QThread::currentThreadId in the cache key).

7. Add a fetchExport() async method for large PDF/Excel generation that runs exportTableToPdf/exportTableToExcel work in a background thread instead of blocking the GUI thread. The result should be a file path delivered via a new exportComplete signal.
8. Add fetchBackup() and fetchRestore() async wrappers around backupDatabase and restoreDatabase to prevent GUI blocking during potentially long I/O operations.

9. Ensure page destruction safety: in DatabaseTask::run(), after QMetaObject::invokeMethod, if invocation fails (receiver destroyed), simply discard the result. The QPointer pattern or checking the receiver with QMetaObject::invokeMethod return value handles this.
10. Add loading state tracking: a Q_PROPERTY(QVariantMap loadingStates READ loadingStates NOTIFY loadingStatesChanged) that QML can bind to, tracking which report types are currently loading.
acceptance_criteria: Every fetchX() method increments a generation counter and passes it to DatabaseTask
Stale results (generation mismatch) are silently discarded in the onXTaskFinished slots
No duplicate concurrent requests of the same type are submitted (second call cancels the first via generation)
DatabaseTask includes timing data (request_start, db_start, db_end, transform_start, transform_end) in the result
A reportTimingComplete signal exists and is emitted with timing data for every completed report
Export operations (PDF/Excel) can run asynchronously via fetchExport()
QML pages can bind to loadingStates to show loading indicators
Code compiles with no warnings related to threading (verified via CI)
No QSqlDatabase is shared across threads - each DatabaseTask uses services that create their own connections
verification: Run qmllint on all QML files to confirm no QML errors
Validate CMakeLists.txt includes any new source files
grep for QThreadPool::globalInstance()->start to confirm all async methods use the pattern
grep to confirm generation_ tracking exists in all fetchX methods
findings: Implementation complete. All 14 async operations (10 original reports + transactions + export + backup + restore) have generation tracking, timing instrumentation, and QPointer safety. The DatabaseTask enum was extended from 10 to 14 members. Slot signatures changed to include (int generation, QVariantMap/QVariantList result, QVariantMap timings) but signal signatures for QML bindings remain unchanged (backward compatible). The loadingStates property is a QVariantMap with 14 bool keys. Linux sandbox cannot run qmllint or compile - CI on Windows validates compilation.
